choices: one is nominative, one is vocative

hints.it is a scribal error: The repetition is intentional in the Vulgate — it conveys urgency and affection
hints.one is nominative, one is vocative: Both 'Martha' are vocative — the repetition adds emphasis
hints.it indicates two different people: Both refer to the same Martha — the repetition is for rhetorical emphasis

hints.to eat: That would be 'edere' — 'es' here is from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to go: That would be 'ire' — 'es' is from 'esse' (to be)
hints.to know: That would be 'scire' — 'es' is from 'esse' (to be)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'sis' — 'es' is present indicative
hints.imperfect indicative: Imperfect would be 'eras' — 'es' is present indicative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'eris' — 'es' is present indicative

hints.edo, esse, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat)
hints.eo, ire, ii, itum: These are the parts of 'ire' (to go)
hints.exsto, exstare, exstiti, —: These are the parts of 'exstare' (to stand out)

steps.reveal: sollicita = anxious/worried (nom. fem. sg., predicate adj.)

hints.accusative: Accusative feminine would be 'sollicitam' — 'sollicita' is nominative predicate
hints.ablative: Ablative feminine would be 'sollicita', but here it is predicate nominative with 'es'
hints.vocative: Vocative would be for direct address — 'sollicita' describes Martha, predicate nominative

steps.target: to be troubled / to be disturbed
choices: to be troubled / to be disturbed

hints.to turn: That would be 'vertere' — 'turbaris' comes from 'turbari' (to be troubled)
hints.to stir up: That would be the active 'turbare' — 'turbaris' is passive, 'to be troubled'

hints.present indicative active: Active would be 'turbas' — 'turbaris' with -ris is passive
hints.present subjunctive passive: Subjunctive passive would be 'turberis' — 'turbaris' with -a- is indicative (1st conj.)
hints.perfect indicative passive: Perfect passive would be 'turbata es' — 'turbaris' is present passive

hints.turbo, turbinis, m. (noun): That is the noun 'turbo' (whirlwind) — the verb is 'turbo, turbare'
hints.verto, vertere, verti, versum: These are the parts of 'vertere' (to turn)
hints.perturbo, perturbare, perturbavi, pertur: These are the parts of 'perturbare' (to thoroughly disturb) — a compound of 'turbare'

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension names end in -us — 'Martha' ends in -a (1st declension)
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension names have -is in genitive — 'Martha' follows -a pattern
hints.indeclinable: 'Martha' does decline in Latin: Martha, Marthae, etc.

translation: Martha, Martha, you are anxious and troubled
